source.section: 2 - Air-to-Ground Weapons > 2.9 - AGM-88C HARM Anti-Radiation Missile > 2.9.1 - AGM-88C HARM (TOO)

steps.voiced: Set Master Arm switch to ARM.

steps.action: Set the TAC EW (Early Warning) page on the right DDI and the SMS (Stores Management System) page on the left DDI.
steps.voiced: Set TAC EW on the right DDI and SMS on the left DDI.

steps.action: In SMS (Stores Management System) page, click on the OSB next to HARM to select it
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to HARM to select it.

steps.action: Set Sensor Control Switch to the HARM page DDI (Sensor Control Switch LEFT since we showed up the HARM page on the left DDI). A diamond will appear to show the left DDI is selected.
steps.voiced: Set Sensor Control Switch toward the HARM page DDI — confirm diamond appears.

steps.action: Select which emitter you want to track by pressing the RAID / FLIR FOV Button (“I” binding).
steps.voiced: Select the desired emitter using the RAID/FLIR FOV Button.

steps.action: Fly align target icon with center cross seeker head. (Note: The HARM Display page is not a Top-Down view; it's a POV (Point-of-View) of the HARM seeker) head with no ground stabilization. HARM DSPLY format provides a boresight view of the HARM seeker.
steps.voiced: Fly to align the target icon with the HARM seeker center cross.

steps.action: Press the Cage/Uncage Button to uncage the HARM and set it to HANDOFF (H-OFF) Mode. When uncaged, the missile will attempt to lock onto the selected target within the seeker center. The HARM has a range of up to 80 nm.
steps.voiced: Press the Cage/Uncage Button to uncage HARM and enter HANDOFF mode.

steps.action: When lock is acquired by the HARM, the HARM crossed-out indication will disappear. Hold the Weapon Release Button (« RALT+SPACE ») to fire missile.
steps.voiced: Confirm HARM crossed-out clears when lock acquired — hold Weapon Release Button to fire.
